data.title: Maple CEO brings Wall Street-style institutional lending on-chain with Bitcoin-backed loans
data.description:
Sid Powell, CEO and co-founder of Maple, is bringing an inspired Wall Street lending model to the crypto sphere, using Bitcoin as the basis.
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/maple-ceo-pioneers-btc-backed-lending/

data.title: Ethereum Whale Faces Liquidation Risk Amid Price Drop
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, on-chain analyst Yu Jin has reported that a significant Ethereum holder is at risk of liquidation. The whale, holding 67,000 ETH, is just $100 away from the liquidation price of their Ethereum position.

Earlier this month, the whale's position was nearly liquidated due to a drop in ETH prices. However, they managed to avoid liquidation by repaying some of their debt and reducing their position. Following this, a rebound in ETH prices temporarily alleviated the risk of liquidation.

Despite the temporary relief, the whale has not taken any further actions to lower the liquidation price, such as adding more collateral or reducing their position. With ETH prices nearing previous lows, another $100 drop could trigger the liquidation of their holdings.

data.title: The whale holding 67,000 ETH is on the verge of liquidation. The liquidation price of its ETH position is only $100
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to the monitoring of on-chain analyst Yu Jin, the whale holding 67,000 ETH is on the verge of liquidation, and the liquidation price of its ETH position is only $100 away. On March 11, the whale's 67,000 ETH position was on the verge of liquidation due to the decline in ETH prices. He saved his position from liquidation by repaying and reducing his position. Later, the rebound of ETH temporarily freed his position from liquidation risk, and there was no operation to increase margin, reduce positions, etc. to reduce the liquidation price. If ETH falls by another $100, his position will be liquidated.

data.title: Trusted AI project Privasea opens official eSIM reservations today
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to official news, the trusted AI project Privasea has opened official eSIM reservations today. It is reported that this eSIM will be combined with its flagship product ImHuman, and will provide global digital citizens with identity privacy, communication, payment and other integrated services.

data.title: Cryptocurrency Market Sentiment Declines Amid Fear and Greed Index Drop
data.description: According to PANews, the cryptocurrency Fear and Greed Index has significantly decreased to 26, down from 44 the previous day, indicating a shift to a panic level in market sentiment. The index, which ranges from 0 to 100, is calculated based on several factors: volatility (25%), market trading volume (25%), social media trends (15%), market surveys (15%), Bitcoin's dominance in the market (10%), and Google search trends (10%).

data.title: Galaxy Digital Faces Legal Action Over Terra Promotion Under New York's Martin Act
data.description:
According to Cointelegraph, the New York State Attorney General (NAYG) has initiated legal proceedings against Galaxy Digital, citing its promotional activities related to the now-defunct cryptocurrency Terra (LUNA). This move has been criticized by SkyBridge Capital founder Anthony Scaramucci, who described it as an unfair use of the legal system. Scaramucci expressed his concerns in a March 28 post on X, labeling the action as 'LAWFARE' due to the Martin Act, a powerful New York law that he believes is prone to misuse.

The Martin Act is recognized as one of the most stringent anti-fraud and securities laws in the United States, granting prosecutors the ability to pursue financial fraud cases without the necessity of proving intent. The NAYG has accused Galaxy Digital of violating this act through its alleged promotion of Terra, leading to a $200 million settlement agreement. Documents filed by the NAYG on March 24 reveal that Galaxy Digital acquired 18.5 million LUNA tokens at a 30% discount in October 2020, subsequently promoting and selling them without adhering to disclosure regulations.

Anthony Scaramucci defended Galaxy CEO Michael Novogratz, stating that Novogratz was under the impression that his statements about Luna were accurate, as he had been misled by Terraform Labs and its former CEO, Do Kwon. Meanwhile, Keith Grossman, president of enterprise at MoonPay, admitted to being unfamiliar with the Martin Act, resorting to AI chatbot ChatGPT for clarification. Grossman described the act as broad and essentially embodying the concept of lawfare, expressing sympathy for Novogratz's predicament.

The legal filing alleges that Galaxy Digital played a role in boosting the market price of LUNA from $0.31 in October 2020 to $119.18 in April 2022, profiting significantly in the process. Asset manager and investor Anthony Pompliano, while not well-versed in the lawsuit's specifics, vouched for Novogratz's character, describing him as a 'good man' committed to philanthropy. The collapse of Terra remains one of the most notorious failures in the cryptocurrency sector. In March 2024, SEC attorney Devon Staren characterized Terra as a 'house of cards' during proceedings in the US District Court for the Southern District of New York, highlighting its downfall for investors in 2022.

data.title: Monad Testnet Sees Significant Increase in Address Count
data.description: According to Foresight News, on-chain data reveals that the Monad testnet has reached a total of 73,433,692 addresses. In the past 24 hours, the network has added 3,605,685 new addresses.

data.title: Justin Sun: TRON will continue to work with WLFI to promote the adoption of cryptocurrency
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News Zach Witkoff, co-founder of the Trump family's crypto project World Liberty Financial (WLFI), said in an interview recently that he would work with TRON to promote the adoption of cryptocurrencies. In response, Sun Yuchen, founder of TRON and member of the Huobi HTX Global Advisory Committee, reposted on the X platform, saying that he was pleased to see Zach Witkoff's emphasis on the importance of stablecoins and his sense of mission to integrate them into the American business sector. Sun Yuchen said, "TRON will continue to work with WLFI to promote the adoption of cryptocurrencies - as we do globally - to provide infrastructure that enables millions of people every day to use blockchain technology, enable p2p transactions and seamlessly access the world's reserve currency, the US dollar."
